title: Hydrogel retained-state transport memory
summary: A thermal, chemical, or swelling protocol writes a retained hydration or microstructural state. After the writing condition is removed, that state changes later transport, release, opacity, modulus, or conductance.
invariant: A prior input writes an internal state; after the input is removed, the state changes a later response through a closure law and a boundary condition.
equations: partial_t phi_w = nabla cdot(D_w(m,T) nabla phi_w)
variables: m(x,t): retained hydration or frustrated microstructure
T(t): thermal writing protocol
D_w(m,T): memory-dependent water diffusion coefficient
B: gel boundary, bath, surface, or device geometry
y(t): swelling, opacity, release, modulus, conductance, or flux
controls: same final temperature with different write duration
references: community: add field-specific DOI/arXiv references here

title: Active droplet interfacial memory
summary: A chemical exposure writes an interfacial or near-field state. After transfer or removal of the writing condition, the retained state changes droplet motion through interfacial stress and force balance.
invariant: A prior input writes an internal state; after the input is removed, the state changes a later response through a closure law and a boundary condition.
equations: partial_t Gamma + nabla_S cdot(Gamma u_S)=k_ads c(Gamma_max-Gamma)-k_des Gamma+D_Gamma nabla_S^2 Gamma
variables: c(x,t): chemical concentration field
Gamma(s,t): interfacial coverage state
m(t): retained interfacial or near-field memory
B: bath, wall, channel, or surface boundary
X(t), U(t): droplet position and velocity
references: community: add field-specific DOI/arXiv references here

title: Bioelectric state controls later tissue repair
summary: A transient electrical, molecular, or wound signal writes a retained cellular or tissue state. Later repair, morphology, or fate is predicted by that state after the original signal is removed.
invariant: A prior input writes an internal state; after the input is removed, the state changes a later response through a closure law and a boundary condition.
variables: q(t) or q(x,t): retained cellular, bioelectric, immune, or developmental state
u(t): morphogen, metabolite, electrical, mechanical, immune, or neural signal
B: membrane, tissue niche, wound boundary, organoid compartment, or assay boundary
y(t): phenotype, migration, secretion, repair, morphology, or fate response
controls: same input with altered tissue boundary
same final state with different history
references: community: add field-specific DOI/arXiv references here

title: Gene regulatory feedback state
summary: A regulatory signal changes an internal network state. Feedback and closure determine whether the state remains admissible and produces a later phenotype.
invariant: A protocol updates an internal state under closure constraints; later output depends on the updated state rather than only on the current input.
variables: q_n: regulatory network state
u_n: perturbation, signal, or treatment
B: cell type, niche, compartment, or assay boundary
y_n: fate, expression pattern, phenotype, or secretion
controls: same final input with different history
references: community: add field-specific DOI/arXiv references here

title: Collective trace-mediated route choice
summary: Local agents write an environmental trace. Later agents read the trace through boundary and update rules, producing route choice, allocation, or collective action.
invariant: A prior input writes an internal or environmental state; after the input is removed, the state changes a later response through a closure law and a boundary condition.
variables: m_t: retained trace, memory, record, or environmental mark
pi_t: agent or group policy state
u_t: signal, task cue, reward, or local interaction
B: network, platform, arena, institution, or task boundary
action_t: route choice, consensus, allocation, cascade, or group response
controls: same final trace with shuffled history
references: community: add field-specific DOI/arXiv references here

title: Institutional rule memory and delayed action
summary: A rule, record, or precedent stores a collective state. Later decisions are constrained by that retained state and by the boundary of the institution or platform.
invariant: A protocol updates an internal state under closure constraints; later output depends on the updated state rather than only on the current input.
variables: m_t: rule, memory, record, precedent, or shared belief
u_t: event, signal, proposal, or incentive
B: institution, platform, network, or governance boundary
a_t: decision, allocation, consensus, sanction, or collective action
measurements: prediction residual under erased rule history
controls: same current input with different institutional history
references: community: add field-specific DOI/arXiv references here
